notes: Pipeline gap fixed: data/universe.json had never been built since the 06-15 strategy pivot, leaving 4 straight sessions with an empty watchlist. Ran scripts/build_universe.py (516 tickers: 503 S&P 500 + 101 NASDAQ-100, deduplicated) -- required two fixes: Wikipedia now blocks requests without a User-Agent header (added one), and the NASDAQ-100 table parse choked on a non-string-column table (made the ticker-column lookup defensive). evening_scan.py also had a latent bug: one invalid symbol (BF-B, and BRK-B) failing inside a 50-symbol batch was killing all 50 quotes for that batch every run since chunks are deterministic and alphabetical -- added a drop-invalid-and-retry loop, recovering coverage from 466/516 to 514/516 tickers and surfacing a third candidate (CDNS) that the bug had been silently hiding. Of the 3 survivors: MGM is the strongest setup (RS 1.30 well above the 1.10 floor, vol dry-up to 0.48 is excellent, but VCP 0.72 and 7.0% off the 52w high are both near the edges of their thresholds rather than deep in ideal territory). CDNS is mid-tier (RS 1.12 just clears the floor, VCP 0.74 and vol-dry 0.67 reasonable). ANET is the weakest of the three -- RS 1.16 is only modestly above threshold, VCP 0.79 and vol-dry 0.87 are both close to their respective cutoffs (0.80 and 0.90), meaning compression and seller exhaustion are barely qualifying rather than convincing. None of the three 52w-high distances (6.5-7.0%) look like a spike day; each closed mildly below a clean prior-day high. Market context: SPY closed -0.54% today (VIX 16.1, calm), so these setups are coiling against a soft tape -- entry agent should weight the breakout-confirmation gate carefully tomorrow given the recent SPY pullback.

weekly_observations: All three trades happened on Monday June 15; Tuesday through Friday produced zero qualifying entries. The one genuine edge confirmed this week: SNDK at RSI 59 with ATR% 0.338% — the only name to extend beyond the stop, hitting the +2 ATR partial target and trailing out at +1.73%, netting +88.47 for the round trip. TSEM (RSI 64, ATR% 0.285%) noise-stopped 33 seconds post-entry, the 5th straight occurrence of the sub-0.30%-ATR hair-trigger pattern — the widened 0.70% floor has failed as a fix, so ATR% >= 0.30% must become a hard filter at entry. The scan universe remained very thin (0.4-0.6% pass rate all week), reflecting a market in mid-recovery rather than broad accumulation; patience and strict filter discipline are the right posture heading into next week, with the ATR floor upgrade being the single highest-leverage change to implement before Monday.
